result: Systematic shared mappings between acoustic continua and basic tastes/concentrations

population: adult nonsynesthetes; exact N not stated in PubMed abstract
result: Sweet/sour preferentially matched with high pitch; umami/bitter with low pitch
limitation: Explicit matching and familiarity/hedonics may drive results

citation: Reinoso Carvalho; Wang; van Ee; Persoone; Spence (2017), Appetite 108:383-390
design: randomized repeated tasting of identical chocolate
population: participants tasted identical chocolate twice; exact N not stated in PubMed abstract
result: Creamy soundtrack increased rated creaminess and sweetness versus rough soundtrack
limitation: Food remained present; no evidence sound generated taste alone

population: healthy participants; verify full text before quoting N
result: Music-evoked autobiographical memory tracked with medial prefrontal activity
limitation: Familiar music differs from novel procedural signatures; fMRI inference does not transfer to UMBRAL
relevance: Supports music-memory link not learned novel cue

citation: Kershaw; Running (2018), Archives of Oral Biology 92:90-95
design: classical conditioning with visual cue predicting sour candy
population: human participants; exact N not stated in accessible abstract
result: Greater saliva weight for candy-paired image and increase across first visit
limitation: Salivation is not taste; visual cue and candy do not establish auditory transfer

citation: Avery; Carrington; Martin (2023), Progress in Neurobiology 223:102423
design: high-field fMRI with imagined tastes and food pictures

result: Quality-specific patterns for imagined and inferred tastes overlapped in dorsal mid-insula
limitation: Neural overlap does not show subjective equivalence or auditory causation
